message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should charge 100G for 4 runes (one block of 3 plus 1 single)"
Expected: { premium: 115 } for 4 runes newcomer (4×25=100 base + 10 first-ins + 5 fee = 115)
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

The spec rule: block applies ONLY when the count is EXACTLY 3 alike components. For any other count (1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, ...) it's individual pricing N × 25.

Update `componentGroupBase`:
- if items.length === 3 → COMPONENT_BLOCK_BASE (60)
- else → items.length × itemBase

Verify all 15 tests still pass:
- 1 rune → 25 base ✓
- 2 runes → 50 base ✓
- 3 runes → 60 (block) ✓
- 4 runes → 100 (no block) ✓ (this test)
- (later: 7 runes → 175; 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 120)

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal change to make the failing test pass.

content.text:
All 15 tests pass. The minimal change works.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Changed `componentGroupBase` to apply the block discount only when `items.length === 3` exactly; otherwise use individual pricing (`length × itemBase`).
**Result**: All 15 tests pass (34 todo remaining)
**Approach**: Minimal conditional — single `if` check for the exact block size, falling back to `N × itemBase` for any other count. This satisfies the spec rule that the block applies only for exactly 3 alike components.

File modified: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts
